description_long: This house is a modern, boxy residential building with three visible stories, featuring a flat roof and a minimalist facade. The exterior is composed of alternating dark and light panels, with large rectangular windows distributed asymmetrically across each level. The front side includes a recessed entryway and a balcony with a glass railing, adding to the contemporary aesthetic. The windows vary in size, with some spanning nearly the full height of a story, while others are smaller and more discrete. The structure appears to be constructed primarily from concrete or masonry, with glass and metal accents. The overall design emphasizes clean lines, geometric forms, and functional outdoor spaces.
description: A three-story modern house with a flat roof, dark and light paneling, large asymmetrical windows, and a glass balcony. The design is minimalist and geometric, with a recessed entryway and contemporary materials.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway, large windows, and a glass balcony on the right side.
description_view_1: Side view with fewer windows, highlighting the alternating panel design and the solid, blocky structure.
description_view_2: Rear view with a more closed-off appearance, featuring smaller windows and minimal exterior detailing.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing additional windows, the balcony from a different angle, and the overall boxy form.
attribute_entryway_design: Recessed main entry with a simple door, no porch, and a glass balcony above.
